_note: 2026-07-22 batch (batch 1 of an ongoing 100+-program research pass): 15 programs verified against a live query of the current DB (400 programs, 90 tags, dumped read-only before research) to avoid re-adding programs already covered -- this repo's existing mechinot/hesder/archaeology coverage turned out to be extremely thorough, so most candidates researched this round were confirmed duplicates and are NOT in this file (see the rejected-candidates list shared with the user alongside this batch, not stored in a data file). Every signupUrl/contactWebsite below was hit with curl (browser User-Agent, since masaisrael.org and a few others block bare curl) and returned HTTP 200 on 2026-07-22. No contactEmail was guessed -- only values actually observed on an official page are included, per this repo's provenance rule; import-researched.ts will route them to data/ignored-import-emails-<date>.json rather than Program.contactEmail, same as every prior batch. No cost/tuition data included except where explicitly and publicly stated on the program's own site. Tags used below are all pre-existing (verified against a fresh DB tag dump before writing this file) -- no new tag values introduced.

archaeology_digs.name: El-Araj (Bethsaida-Julias) Excavation Project
archaeology_digs.organization: Kinneret Institute for Galilean Archaeology (Kinneret College) / Nyack College
archaeology_digs.location: El-Araj (Beit HaBek), northern shore of the Sea of Galilee, Israel
archaeology_digs.description: An ongoing excavation on the northern shore of the Sea of Galilee investigating the site identified by the project as ancient Bethsaida-Julias, offering short volunteer dig sessions to students and adult volunteers.
archaeology_digs.durationText: 2-week sessions (three offered in 2026: Jul 10-24, Jul 24-Aug 7, and a fall session Oct 9-23)
archaeology_digs.signupInstructions: Contact the excavation directors directly or apply via the project's official site.
archaeology_digs.adminNote: Co-directed by Prof. Mordechai Aviam (Kinneret College) and Prof. R. Steven Notley (Nyack College). Confirmed distinct from the already-listed Hazor, Hippos-Sussita, En-Gedi, Abel Beth Maacah, Akko, Gezer, Megiddo, and Shimron excavations -- no overlap in site, directors, or organization.

archaeology_digs.name: Legio Excavation (Jezreel Valley Regional Project)
archaeology_digs.description: A field school excavating Legio, the base of the Roman Sixth Legion near Tel Megiddo, run by the Jezreel Valley Regional Project with academic credit available.
archaeology_digs.signupInstructions: Register via the Jezreel Valley Regional Project website; academic credit is available.
archaeology_digs.adminNote: Shares directors and parent project (Jezreel Valley Regional Project) with the already-listed 'Tel Megiddo Excavation' but is a separate dig site with its own 2026 dates (Jun 2-Jul 23, vs. Megiddo's Jun 27-Jul 23) and its own registration page -- flagging as related rather than a duplicate for reviewer judgment. Also worth noting: the existing 'Tel Megiddo Excavation' entry's signupUrl/contactWebsite (a Biblical Archaeology Society listing page and a Facebook page) are weaker sources than the dedicated themegiddoexpedition.com site found during this research -- not fixed here since editing an existing entry is outside this batch's scope, but flagging for a future edit pass.

archaeology_digs.name: Tel Shaddud Archaeological Project
archaeology_digs.organization: Tel Aviv University / Israel Antiquities Authority / Ruhr University Bochum / Friedrich-Schiller University Jena
archaeology_digs.location: Jezreel Valley, northern Israel (dormitory housing in Nahalal)
archaeology_digs.description: A joint Israeli-German excavation in the Jezreel Valley investigating the transition from Canaanite urbanism to the early Israelite monarchy, open to international student and volunteer participants.
archaeology_digs.signupInstructions: Register through the project website; scholarships are available for eligible students.
archaeology_digs.adminNote: As of this research date (2026-07-22), the 2026 international-team registration deadline (Jun 1) had already passed; only local-volunteer registration remained open. Included as a currently-active, verifiably live program (site and season both confirmed real) rather than for guaranteed 2026 international enrollment -- worth re-checking registration timing before outreach.

mechinot.name: Mechinat Derech Avot
mechinot.description: A pre-military mechina under the Bnei David (Eli) network specifically for young men of Ethiopian-Israeli descent, combining Torah and Beta Israel heritage study with physical training and leadership preparation before IDF service.
mechinot.signupInstructions: Contact the program directly through the Bnei David network's site.
mechinot.adminNote: Distinct target population/site from the already-listed 'Mechinat Bnei David (Eli)' but shares the same parent network/organization -- flagging as related, not duplicate. Founded 2019 for Ethiopian-Israeli olim youth specifically.

mechinot.name: Mechinat Maale Ephraim
mechinot.description: An advanced-track mechina of the Bnei David (Eli) network located in the Jordan Valley settlement of Ma'ale Efrayim, combining advanced Torah study with agricultural work and pre-military preparation for second-year students and military veterans.
mechinot.signupInstructions: Contact the program directly through the Bnei David network's site.
mechinot.adminNote: Also part of the Bnei David (Eli) network, like 'Mechinat Derech Avot' above and the already-listed 'Mechinat Bnei David (Eli)' -- a distinct location/track (Jordan Valley, second-year/veteran cohort), flagged as related, not duplicate. This yeshiva is also independently listed on the Union of Hesder Yeshivas' directory as a hesder institution, suggesting it may run parallel mechina and hesder tracks -- only the mechina track is captured here.

mechinot.name: Mechinat Galil Elyon
mechinot.location: Kibbutz Amir and other Upper Galilee kibbutzim, Israel
mechinot.description: A pre-military leadership academy operating across several Upper Galilee kibbutzim, with year-long, 18-month, and half-year tracks combining leadership training, community volunteering, and preparation for IDF service.
mechinot.signupInstructions: Israeli applicants register at rishum.app/gius-galil-ep; international applicants use a separate Google Form linked from the official site.
mechinot.adminNote: Founded 1998; explicitly maintains a separate international-applicant registration path, distinct from every other secular mechina currently in the DB.

mechinot.name: HaMechina HaMadait (Scientific Pre-Military Academy)
mechinot.description: A pre-military academy for science-oriented high school graduates that combines advanced coursework in fields such as astrophysics and neuroscience with community science outreach and preparation for IDF service.
mechinot.signupInstructions: Apply via the national pre-military academies registration portal (portal.mechinot.org.il), linked from the program's own site.
mechinot.adminNote: Run in partnership with the Davidson Institute of Science Education (Weizmann Institute); Ofakim branch established 2019, Netivot branch 2024.

mechinot.name: Mechinat Ruach HaSadeh
mechinot.description: A religious pre-military mechina for young men at Kibbutz Be'erot Yitzhak that combines Torah study with an open religious-Zionist outlook encouraging critical thinking, alongside preparation for IDF service.
mechinot.signupInstructions: Apply via the registration/screening link on the program's official site.
mechinot.adminNote: Confirmed distinct from the already-listed 'Mechinat Ruach Nachon' -- different Hebrew name ('spirit of the field' vs. 'correct spirit'), different kibbutz, different official site.

mechinot.name: Mechinat HaShomer HaChadash
mechinot.location: Israel (agricultural farm site; branch location not fully confirmed -- see note)
mechinot.description: Israel's first agricultural pre-military mechina, combining hands-on farm work with Bible study, Zionism seminars, and preparation for IDF service, run by the HaShomer HaChadash movement.
mechinot.signupInstructions: Apply via the form on the program's official page.
mechinot.adminNote: The fetched page content was inconsistent between a general description ('first agricultural pre-military mechina in Israel') and specific per-cohort details (a 'Ron farm, Arad' location and a shinshin@hashomer.org.il contact address, which reads more like a gap-year shnat-sherut program than a mechina) -- location and contact details should be independently re-verified with the organization before any outreach or before treating them as confirmed.

mechinot.name: HaMechina HaChadasha BeLod (The New Academy in Lod)
mechinot.description: A yeshiva-style pre-military mechina in Lod combining intensive Torah study with physical training, wilderness experience, and preparation for IDF service.
mechinot.signupInstructions: Attend an open/trial weekend, then an individual interview with the admissions team.
mechinot.adminNote: Confirmed as a distinct organization from the already-listed 'Mechinat Maoz (Lod)' -- separate founding history and separate official website (mechinat-lod.co.il vs. mechinatmaoz.wixsite.com/mechina).

hesder_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat HaHesder Yeruham
hesder_yeshivot.description: A hesder yeshiva in the Negev town of Yeruham, founded in 1993, combining advanced Torah study with IDF service.
hesder_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva directly via its official site.

hesder_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Har Shalom (Mitzpe Ashtamoa)
hesder_yeshivot.location: Mitzpe Ashtamoa, southern Mount Hebron, Israel
hesder_yeshivot.description: A hesder yeshiva originally founded in the settlement of Chomesh in 2002 and relocated to Mitzpe Ashtamoa in southern Mount Hebron after Chomesh's evacuation, combining Torah study with IDF service.
hesder_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact via the Union of Hesder Yeshivas' listing page.
hesder_yeshivot.adminNote: No independent yeshiva-run website was found despite searching; signupUrl points to the Union of Hesder Yeshivas' directory listing for this institution, the best verified live source available -- same precedent as the existing 'Tel Megiddo Excavation' entry, which also uses a third-party listing page rather than a dedicated org site.

hesder_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat HaHesder Karmiel
hesder_yeshivot.description: A hesder yeshiva in the northern city of Karmiel, founded in 2003, offering both a hesder track combining Torah study with IDF service and a standard higher-yeshiva (yeshiva gevoha) track.
hesder_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva directly via its official site.

hesder_yeshivot.name: Yeshivat Neve Dekalim (Ashdod)
hesder_yeshivot.description: A hesder yeshiva originally founded in Yamit and later re-established in Neve Dekalim, Gush Katif; relocated to Ashdod after the 2005 disengagement, combining Torah study with IDF service.
hesder_yeshivot.signupInstructions: Contact the yeshiva directly via its official site.

masa_and_volunteer_programs.name: Masa Magen David Adom EMT Volunteer Program
masa_and_volunteer_programs.organization: Masa Israel Journey / Magen David Adom
masa_and_volunteer_programs.description: A Masa-administered program in which participants complete an EMT certification course and then serve active ambulance shifts alongside Magen David Adom crews, gaining hands-on emergency medical experience.
masa_and_volunteer_programs.cost: $400-$500 program fee; a fixed stipend is provided
masa_and_volunteer_programs.signupInstructions: Apply through the Masa Israel website.
masa_and_volunteer_programs.adminNote: Distinct from the already-listed 'MDA Overseas Volunteers Program' -- this is specifically the Masa-administered EMT-certification-plus-shift track (ages 18-40, ~$400 stipend, Haifa/Be'er Sheva cohorts only), a different intake channel/structure than the existing entry even though both ultimately place volunteers with Magen David Adom. Flagging as related, not duplicate, for reviewer judgment.
